items.title: State the positive class and threshold
items.avoid: Publishing precision/recall without saying which label is positive or what score cutoff defines a prediction.
items.prefer: Document `pos_label`, threshold (e.g. 0.5 or cost-optimal), and whether scores or hard labels were used.
items.why: Metrics are undefined for reviewers without the operating point.
items.example: Report: 'Fraud as positive, threshold 0.42 chosen on validation for precision ≥ 0.85.'

items.title: Report support with per-class metrics
items.avoid: Quoting F1 = 0.95 on a class with three test examples.
items.prefer: Include support counts in tables; widen evaluation sets for rare classes.
items.why: Small denominators make precision and recall volatile.
items.example: classification_report shows support=7 for 'chargeback' alongside P/R/F1.

items.title: Match metrics to business error costs
items.avoid: Defaulting to F1 when false negatives cost 10× false positives.
items.prefer: Optimize the metric or cost-weighted objective reflecting real penalties; use F1 when errors are similarly harmful.
items.why: Harmonic mean F1 is not a universal utility function.
items.example: Cancer screen: set threshold on validation to recall ≥ 0.95, then report precision at that point.

items.title: Evaluate on temporally honest holdouts
items.avoid: Random splits on time-series fraud or churn where future labels leak into training-like periods.
items.prefer: Time-based or group-based splits; report P/R/F1 on the future window you will deploy into.
items.why: Inflated recall on shuffled i.i.d. splits misstates production catch rate.
items.example: Train through Q1, validate threshold in Q2, report test metrics on Q3 only.
